content: English Adept is a vocabulary pack for
people who already speak English well
and want to speak it better.
Most of these words will be familiar.
You've read them, heard them, maybe even
used them. The goal isn't to learn them.
It's to own them — to understand them
well enough to reach for the right word
at the right moment.
The difference between good English and
great English is rarely about big words.
It's about choosing precisely.
1000 words. Five tiers. Let's begin.
HOW THE PACK IS STRUCTURED
Tier 1 — The foundation.
Common words, sharper understanding.
Tier 2 — Words that unlock the world.
Civic life, professional writing, news,
structured argument.
Tier 3 — Words for thinking clearly.
Logic, cause and effect, nuance.
Tier 4 — What's the difference?
Near-synonyms that aren't the same.
The right tool for the right job.
Tier 5 — The deep end.
Eloquent, evocative, formidable.
TIER 1 — THE FOUNDATION
You probably know most of these words.
The question is whether you use them.
Concede. Volatile. Exempt. Consensus.
Tentative. Redundant. Anecdote.
Words like these sit in the gap between
recognition and use — you understand them
when you read them, but they don't come
to mind when you need them.
Cards include extra detail to help words
stick — word type, etymology, usage notes.
NOTE:
Syn = similar meaning. Ant = opposite.
Here's the unfortunate truth about English:
Using a sophisticated word in the wrong
situation often sounds worse than using
a plain word correctly.
It can be a challenge to express yourself:
Say 'I'm sad' and you sound simple-minded.
Say 'I'm melancholic' in the wrong moment
and you sound ridiculous.
Say 'I'm really down today' and you have
it correct. you sound like a person!
This is register: The word is correct.
The situation is wrong.
Register has three rough zones:
INFORMAL  — home, friends, casual
begin, buy, show, help
NEUTRAL   — most written English,
most professional speech
commence, purchase, display
FORMAL    — legal, academic, official
initiate, procure, exhibit
The vocabulary in this pack lives mostly
in the neutral zone — the register that
works everywhere and offends no one.
When in doubt, neutral wins.
